extraction.one_line_summary: BOL Cees won stage 2 sprint after DSM delivered perfect late leadout around final right-hand corner, while MATTHEWS Michael accumulated enough intermediate sprint bonuses to take overall lead
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo forced echelons before first intermediate sprint despite light wind, isolating KRUIJSWIJK Steven in second group opportunistically
MATTHEWS Michael hunted bonus seconds at both intermediate sprints specifically to steal race lead without winning stage
DSM's BENOOT Tiesj held leadout through final right-hand corner at 200m, letting BOL Cees delay launch until straightaway advantage
PEDERSEN Mads possibly overgeared when acceleration started after corner, losing sprint despite good leadout position

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: systematically collected intermediate sprint bonuses to gain yellow jersey without stage win, revealing GC ambition early
rider_signals.BOL Cees: positive: first win since February 2020 Algarve shows DSM's corner-entry leadout unlocked sprint capability
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: crashed heavily into roadside pole at first intermediate sprint, major injury risk signal
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: negative: caught out in second echelon during Trek's light-wind forcing before intermediate sprint, positioning vulnerability exposed
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: positive: won first intermediate sprint outright, surprising form contradicts declining sprinter narrative
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: good position into finale but possibly overgeared on corner acceleration, tactical execution flaw
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: mentioned among similar sprint finishers without specific placement detail
